html:
Exclusive to:
This content is available only after activating the Lunar Rift

“A carriable container with 6 slots that can hold prepared food.
The food stored in it will be preserved for a long time.”

-

The Polar Bearger Bin is a craftable Item in Don't Starve Together, introduced in From Beyond. It can only be crafted while next to the Brightsmithy. It requires 1 Spark Ark, 1 Thick Fur, 3 Pure Brilliance, and 5 Moon Rock to craft.

The Polar Bearger Bin is a storage unit that can be carried in the inventory, much like the Tackle Box. It can be opened from the inventory. While on the ground, it can be opened or closed, and can be picked up. If you start moving with an open container in your inventory, it will instantly close.

The Polar Bearger Bin can contain and preserve up to 6 stacks of Food Items that were made in a Crock Pot or Portable Crock Pot, including those that are not perishable, such as Jellybeans. It cannot store any other food or items. The spoilage rate of dishes inside the Polar Bearger Bin is multiplied by 0.05, meaning the total spoilage time is multiplied by 20.

As long as the Polar Bearger Bin is closed, any dishes stored inside are safe from the spoiling effect of Toadstool's Spore Clouds.

Contents

  • 1 Skins
  • 2 Trivia
  • 3 Assets
  • 4 Gallery

Skins

Main article: Belongings

  • The Loyal Polar Bloom Bin skin was awarded for watching 3 hours of Don't Starve Together on Twitch from December 19, 2024 to January 16, 2025.
Loyal
Rose Collection
Polar Bloom Bin
Keep things fresh as a frozen rose.
Woven - Elegant
Mystic Collection
Polar Pearl Bin
Preserve your precious morsels.

Trivia

  • The Polar Bearger Bin was introduced in the Host of Horrors update.
  • On beta release prior to update 576123, the Polar Bearger Bin did not drop the Spark Ark when deconstructed.
  • On beta release prior to update 576473, the Polar Bearger Bin reduced spoilage by ten times rather than twenty.
  • On beta release prior to update 576966, the Polar Bearger Bin did not grant dishes immunity to the Spore Cloud's effect.
  • Despite Warly's examination quote, you cannot store crock pot ingredients in the Polar Bearger Bin.

Exclusive to:
This content is available only after activating the Lunar Rift
“There’s still fur on it. Chilling.”
–Wilson
“Can't believe we made a cooler out of you.”
–Willow
“Keep food cold and Wolfgang fed.”
–Wolfgang
“I already carry the coldness within.”
–Wendy
“FLESHLINGS CAN BE USEFUL AFTER ALL”
–WX-78
“A portable chilling unit. Well and good, but cold food makes my teeth ache.”
–Wickerbottom
“Keeps things nice and frosty, eh?”
–Woodie
“Portable indeed. Wilson can carry it.”
–Maxwell
“It will preserve the freshness öf my kills.”
–Wigfrid
“We like our snacks cold.”
–Webber
“This will keep my ingredients fresh.”
–Warly
“Carry belly stuff. Thanks Fuzzy!”
–Wormwood
“Weirdest lunch bucket I've ever seen.”
–Winona
“Is that where the mortals pack food away for the winter?”
–Wortox
“Blegh. Better not get teddy hair in food!”
–Wurt
“We can keep our snacks cold when we go exploring, Woby!”
–Walter
“It can keep my perishables cold anywhere, anytime.”
–Wanda
“A carriable container with 6 slots that can hold prepared food.
The food stored in it will be preserved for a long time.”
-
The Polar Bearger Bin is a craftable Item in Don't Starve Together, introduced in From Beyond. It can only be crafted while next to the Brightsmithy. It requires 1 Spark Ark, 1 Thick Fur, 3 Pure Brilliance, and 5 Moon Rock to craft.
The Polar Bearger Bin is a storage unit that can be carried in the inventory, much like the Tackle Box. It can be opened from the inventory. While on the ground, it can be opened or closed, and can be picked up. If you start moving with an open container in your inventory, it will instantly close.
The Polar Bearger Bin can contain and preserve up to 6 stacks of Food Items that were made in a Crock Pot or Portable Crock Pot, including those that are not perishable, such as Jellybeans. It cannot store any other food or items. The spoilage rate of dishes inside the Polar Bearger Bin is multiplied by 0.05, meaning the total spoilage time is multiplied by 20.
As long as the Polar Bearger Bin is closed, any dishes stored inside are safe from the spoiling effect of Toadstool's Spore Clouds.

Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Polar Bearger Bin was introduced in the Host of Horrors update.
On beta release prior to update 576123, the Polar Bearger Bin did not drop the Spark Ark when deconstructed.
On beta release prior to update 576473, the Polar Bearger Bin reduced spoilage by ten times rather than twenty.
On beta release prior to update 576966, the Polar Bearger Bin did not grant dishes immunity to the Spore Cloud's effect.
Despite Warly's examination quote, you cannot store crock pot ingredients in the Polar Bearger Bin.

The Polar Bearger Bin is a storage unit that can be carried in the inventory, much like the [[Tackle Box]]. It can be opened from the inventory.
